definition: Diet quality describes overall dietary patterns (e.g., high intake of fruits/vegetables vs. highly processed foods) that can influence cardiometabolic, inflammatory, and cancer-related pathways at the population level. GENARCH uses diet quality as an educational exposure category relevant to gene–environment interaction research.
name: Diet Quality (Dietary Pattern)

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Early-life nutrition can shape metabolic development and long-term cohort-level outcomes.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Dietary patterns can influence insulin sensitivity, lipid metabolism, and adiposity-related signaling.
Diet can modulate inflammatory tone and gut-immune interactions through multiple pathways.
